summary:
The radiolucent areas surrounding vital mandibular teeth indicate chronic inflammatory processes such as periapical granulomas, not acute infections like abscesses. Periapical granulomas develop due to prolonged pulpal stimulation while preserving pulp vitality through tissue remodeling—a process consistent with the patient’s findings. 

Note: Do NOT include any commentary about how or why you reached this conclusion—only present the summary itself.

The radiolucent areas around vital mandibular teeth suggest chronic inflammatory lesions such as periapical granulomas. These granulomas form from long-standing pulpal irritation and preserve pulp vitality by promoting adaptive tissue changes, distinguishing them from destructive acute abscesses.
